2606.26874.title: TAVR-VLM: Risk-Conditioned Causal Grounding for Hallucination-Resistant Report Generation
2606.26874.paper_summary: Transcatheter Aortic Valve Replacement (TAVR) planning requires meticulous multimodal reasoning. However, adapting Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) to this high-stakes domain is severely impeded by diagnostic hallucinations, where generated text lacks anatomical grounding. To address this, TAVR-VLM is introduced: a novel framework featuring Risk-Conditioned Causal Grounding Attention (R-CGA) that instantiates a model-internal ``Risk $\rightarrow$ Region $\rightarrow$ Word'' structural grounding pathway. R-CGA compresses multimodal inputs into a causal risk bottleneck, purifying dense visual features into a global risk mask. During autoregressive generation, a support-projected causal consistency objective constrains token-level grounding within the risk-defined support mask. Evaluated on $\text{M}^3\text{TAVR}$, a comprehensive 1,482-patient cohort, TAVR-VLM establishes a new state-of-the-art. It achieves an AUROC of 0.896, boosts CIDEr to 0.936, and drastically reduces the hallucination rate to 8.1\%, thereby improving interpretability for evidence-based surgical AI.
2606.26874.paper_summary_zh: 經導管主動脈瓣置換術（TAVR）的規劃需要細緻的多模態推理。然後，將多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）應用於這一高風險領域受到診斷幻覺的嚴重阻礙，生成的文本缺乏解剖學基礎。為了解決這個問題，提出了TAVR-VLM：一個新穎的框架，具有風險條件因果基礎注意力（R-CGA），該框架實現了一個模型內部的“風險 $\rightarrow$ 區域 $\rightarrow$ 單詞”結構基礎路徑。R-CGA將多模態輸入壓縮到因果風險瓶頸，將密集的視覺特徵純化為全局風險掩碼。在自回歸生成過程中，支持投影的因果一致性目標限制了風險定義的支持掩碼內的標記級基礎。經過對1,482名患者的綜合隊列$\text{M}^3\text{TAVR}$的評估，TAVR-VLM建立了新的最先進技術。它達到了0.896的AUROC，將CIDEr提升至0.936，並將幻覺率大幅降低至8.1\%，從而改善了基於證據的外科AI的可解釋性。
2606.26874.author: Zhixiang Lu et.al.
2606.26874.authors: Zhixiang Lu, Xiwei Liu, Sifan Song, Changkai Ji, Anh Nguyen, Jionglong Su, Imran Razzak, Jinfeng Wang
